channel.title: Azure Event Grid on Sujith Quintelier
channel.description: Recent content in Azure Event Grid on Sujith Quintelier

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Event Grid - MQTT v5 Subscription Identifier
item.description:
Azure Event Grid Standard Namespace now generally supports MQTT v5 subscription identifiers. MQTT subscriptions can include an identifier that is delivered with incoming messages for immediate event routing.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Event Grid Subscription Identifiers
item.description:
Azure Event Grid now offers Subscription Identifiers in public preview. This lets MQTT subscribers identify which subscription triggered a delivery, which can simplify client-side processing in more advanced scenarios.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Event Grid releases for April 2026
item.description:
Azure Event Grid namespaces are expanding MQTT capabilities to support more connected, scalable, standards-based real-time solutions. The release is intended to simplify backend integration and improve responsiveness for device experiences.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Event Grid as a destination for Stripe events now in Public Preview
item.description:
Azure Event Grid is now in public preview as a destination for Stripe events. Developers can route Stripe events into Event Grid to build event-driven architectures without managing webhook infrastructure or custom brokers.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Event Grid provides new capabilities for smarter, more secure event-driven architectures
item.description:
Azure Event Grid is adding Public Preview features for event-driven architectures, focused on improved interoperability, control, and security. The announced preview includes cross-tenant webhook delivery and related capabilities for managing large-scale distributed systems.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Event Grid provides new capabilities for smarter, more secure event-driven architectures
item.description:
Azure Event Grid has added several generally available capabilities to improve event-driven system management, interoperability, and control. The announced GA feature shown in the provided content is MQTT Message Ordering, which preserves in-order delivery of MQTT messages.

  • Source: Azure Updates
